pattern: Drop the assumption that every level is full and the same weaving trick still works: collect the next level's nodes as you scan the current one via next, since a parent may contribute zero, one, or two children.
hints: A queue-based level order still works, but arbitrary gaps mean you cannot assume every node has two children the way the perfect-tree trick does.
Use a placeholder node to collect the next level's nodes as you scan the current level through next, appending whichever children actually exist.
When you fall off the end of a level, jump to the placeholder's captured next level and reset the collector before continuing.

edge_inputs.description: one node has only a right child, its sibling only a left
left-only chain of four nodes, no right children anywhere
right-only chain of four nodes, no left children anywhere
asymmetric holes across a level: one parent has only a left child, its neighbor only a right child, forcing the next pointer to skip a slot
root with a single left child and a negative value
root with a single right child and a negative value

stress.description: random arbitrary binary trees with sparse children
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    budget = [max(1, min(size, 40))]
    def build():
        if budget[0] <= 0 or rng.random() < 0.15:
            return None
        budget[0] -= 1
        val = rng.randint(-1000, 1000)
        left = build()
        right = build()
        return [val, left, right]
    tree = build()
    if tree is None:
        return [[]]
    out = []
    queue = [tree]
    while queue:
        node = queue.pop(0)
        if node is None:
            out.append(None)
            continue
        out.append(node[0])
        queue.append(node[1])
        queue.append(node[2])
    while out and out[-1] is None:
        out.pop()
    return [out]
